source.language_note: Rulebook in ZH-TW; quantities restated in SI.

full_prompt: Design the motor mount tube + forward thrust ring assembly for a Class L Rocket Taiwan Cup 2025 university rocket. The competition plan sets Class L motor impulse 4700 N*s, peak thrust 200 kgf (~2000 N), burn time 3.1 s, vehicle GLOW <= 28.5 kg, target apogee 3 km, minimum rail-exit velocity 30 m/s. The motor mount tube is the inner structural tube that holds the solid rocket motor casing and transfers thrust into the airframe. Geometry: motor tube inner diameter 75 mm (fits an AeroTech M-class 75 mm motor), outer diameter 77.6 mm (wall 1.3 mm), length 800 mm; the forward thrust ring is a 6061-T6 aluminium annulus with OD 150 mm (flush to airframe ID), ID 75 mm, thickness 6 mm, bonded to the motor tube's forward end and to the airframe ID with 3M Scotch-Weld 9323 B/A epoxy adhesive; four centering rings of the same 150/75 mm OD/ID geometry but 3 mm thick are spaced along the tube length at 0, 267, 533, and 800 mm from the aft end. Motor tube material is filament-wound glass/epoxy [+-45/0]s layup with equivalent in-plane stiffness Ex = 25 GPa, Ey = 10 GPa, Gxy = 3.5 GPa, nu_xy = 0.30, density 1900 kg/m^3, allowable in-plane tensile stress 350 MPa, compressive 250 MPa, interlaminar shear 40 MPa. Thrust ring material is 6061-T6 aluminium (69 GPa, 0.33, 2700 kg/m^3, 276 MPa yield, 310 MPa UTS). Load cases: LC1 (Peak thrust) — axial compressive force of 2000 N applied at the motor's aft closure transferred through the thrust ring into the airframe, with the airframe bond line modeled as fully fixed at the thrust ring's outer edge; LC2 (Chamber pressure) — internal gauge pressure of 7.0 MPa on the motor tube inner surface (representative of M-class chamber peak) with both ends axially restrained; LC3 (Drag column) — axial compressive force of 600 N at burnout from aerodynamic drag with airframe restrained, worst-case column buckling; LC4 (Landing shock) — 25 g longitudinal shock on the entire assembly with airframe restrained, representative of hard landing. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, aluminium thrust ring max von Mises < 184 MPa (yield/1.5) and composite tube peak stress < 233 MPa (tensile allowable / 1.5); (R2) under LC2, composite tube hoop stress < 233 MPa and interlaminar shear < 26 MPa (40 / 1.5); (R3) under LC3, first-mode linear buckling load factor of the composite tube >= 3.0; (R4) under LC4, no component above its 1.5x safety factor limit, and adhesive bond-line shear < 10 MPa (Scotch-Weld 9323 B/A manual); (R5) total motor mount + thrust ring + centering rings assembly mass < 1.8 kg. Primary verification: linear static structural FEA with orthotropic composite layup for LC1, LC2, LC4; linear buckling eigenvalue for LC3. Thermal (motor case heat), vibration, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly plus a report of total mass, max stress per material per case, buckling load factor for LC3, and bond-line shear under LC4.
short_prompt:
Design the motor mount tube + forward thrust ring assembly for a Class L Rocket Taiwan Cup 2025 university rocket. Class L plan: 4700 N-s total impulse, 2000 N peak thrust, 3.1 s burn, GLOW <= 28.5 kg, 3 km apogee, rail-exit velocity >= 30 m/s.

Envelope: filament-wound glass/epoxy [+-45/0]s motor tube 75 mm ID x 77.6 mm OD (1.3 mm wall) x 800 mm; four 3 mm centering rings at 0/267/533/800 mm; one 6061-T6 forward thrust ring (150 mm OD x 75 mm ID x 6 mm) adhered with Scotch-Weld 9323 B/A; airframe bond edge treated as fully fixed.

Factor regime: FoS 1.5 on allowables; linear-buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 under post-burnout axial drag; adhesive shear allowable 10 MPa.

Load cases.
- Peak thrust: 2000 N axial through the thrust ring.
- Chamber pressure: 7.0 MPa internal on the motor-tube ID (both ends axially restrained).
- Drag column at burnout: 600 N axial compression with airframe restrained.
- Landing shock: 25 g longitudinal on the assembly with airframe restrained.

prompt.title: Rocket Taiwan Cup Class L motor mount
prompt.brief: Composite motor tube + aluminium thrust ring + four centering rings for a Class L 4700 N*s rocket with 2 kN peak thrust, 7 MPa chamber, and 3 km apogee target.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: composite shell + hex solid for rings

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Rulebook does not impose modal req.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Assumes ablative liner protects composite; thermal soak not in scope.
